# Questions in Livestream 46

Live Stream #46: God Is Our Refuge And Strength.

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l80zyabM1gE?t=192" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:12</a> | Are demons fallen angels, dead Nephilim, or something else? | He leans toward identifying demons as fallen angels rather than the spirits of dead Nephilim. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l80zyabM1gE?t=532" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">8:52</a> | How would ancient Egyptians have understood `elohim`, and how does that differ from modern usage? | He says `elohim` is Hebrew, not Egyptian; the Egyptian category for gods or spirits was broader and would cover many kinds of divine beings. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l80zyabM1gE?t=745" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">12:25</a> | Do ANE texts that praise a god as incomparable undermine biblical monotheistic proof texts? | He says no. Similar rhetoric elsewhere is hyperbolic, but biblical monotheistic claims are more explicit and programmatic. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l80zyabM1gE?t=1062" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">17:42</a> | Does the James ossuary's soil prove it came from the Talpiyot tomb? | He says no; the soil argument is too weak to establish that provenance. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l80zyabM1gE?t=1378" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">22:58</a> | Was Moses's Torah later translated or updated because classical Biblical Hebrew is later than Moses? | He says the language history before about 900 BCE is hard to reconstruct, so strong claims here go beyond the evidence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l80zyabM1gE?t=1650" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">27:30</a> | How harshly did the Persians and Greeks treat the Egyptians, and could that fit Ezekiel 29? | He says the Persians were especially harsh, the Greeks comparatively better, and the Persian period helps explain Egypt's reduced status. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l80zyabM1gE?t=2025" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">33:45</a> | Could Moses have written Genesis at Sinai with God simply revealing the past to him? | He says it is possible but not the most likely model; he prefers a more gradual composition rooted in Moses's background and sources. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l80zyabM1gE?t=2236" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">37:16</a> | Is God's name better rendered `Yahweh` or `Yahuwah`, and is `Yahweh` pagan? | He rejects the pagan claim and prefers `Yahweh`, appealing to Semitic phonology and comparative evidence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l80zyabM1gE?t=2454" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">40:54</a> | Can you explain the Genesis 1 cosmic temple theory again? | He presents Genesis 1 as temple-oriented creation language, especially in the imaging of humanity and the ordered sacred space motif. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l80zyabM1gE?t=2734" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">45:34</a> | Did Egypt ever occupy or vassalize Libya or areas farther west like Canaan? | Egypt did not vassalize Libya as it did Canaan or Kush; it mainly guarded the western border with fortresses and garrisons. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l80zyabM1gE?t=3005" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">50:05</a> | Could Socrates' daemon or guiding voice play into the New Testament idea of demons? | Yes, the Greek daemon concept may have influenced terminology, but he frames demons as unclean spirits acting outside their intended order. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l80zyabM1gE?t=3219" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">53:39</a> | Have you ever studied Old Testament prophecies? | Yes; they are complex because of poetry, recontextualization, and possible double meanings. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l80zyabM1gE?t=3438" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">57:18</a> | What are your thoughts on Dr. Schoch's erosion theory about the Sphinx? | He rejects the old-Sphinx water-erosion argument and says Giza wind erosion explains the evidence better. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l80zyabM1gE?t=3691" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:01:31</a> | Did mankind once worship God and then fall away because of idols? | Personally yes; he thinks polytheism can develop by aggregating local worship traditions, though early evidence is limited. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l80zyabM1gE?t=3932" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:05:32</a> | What is your producer's favorite color? | The producer likes blue; he likes red. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l80zyabM1gE?t=4030" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:07:10</a> | Do early Exodus proponents have to disregard archaeological evidence? | Yes; he says they must ignore or reinterpret evidence such as Pi-Ramesses, Pithom, Merneptah, Hazor, chariots, and Late Egyptian vocabulary. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l80zyabM1gE?t=4318" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:11:58</a> | If Exodus numbers are not literal, what about Gideon's army being reduced to 300? | He connects the 300 to an execration-style ritual and says the army is not presented as doing the fighting itself. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l80zyabM1gE?t=4735" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:18:55</a> | Besides Genesis or Exodus, what Old Testament stories do you find most fascinating historically? | David, Goliath's armor and slingstones, and Hezekiah's interactions with Sennacherib. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l80zyabM1gE?t=4948" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:22:28</a> | Why do many Christians insist the world is 6,000 years old, and what alternatives are preferable? | He does not commit to one origins model; he rejects rigid young-earth chronology and says several alternatives exist. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l80zyabM1gE?t=5471" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:31:11</a> | What should I keep in mind while reading an older history of Egypt derived from monuments? | Use caution; monuments alone are incomplete, and he suggests reading alongside a newer synthesis such as Ian Shaw. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l80zyabM1gE?t=5670" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:34:30</a> | Are later cuneiform signs influenced by alphabetic letters or Egyptian scripts? | No clear Egyptian-script influence; Ugaritic is alphabetic cuneiform, and possible alphabetic influence would need closer study. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l80zyabM1gE?t=5971" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:39:31</a> | What is your favorite secular song? | He names Lacuna Coil's "Comalies" and says he likes symphonic goth. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l80zyabM1gE?t=6048" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:40:48</a> | How similar is ancient Egyptian to modern Coptic? | Coptic is the final phase of Egyptian; intelligibility depends on the Egyptian phase, with Coptic sounding slang-like to earlier speakers. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l80zyabM1gE?t=6335" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:45:35</a> | Is Sumerian the oldest language in the world? | Sumerian and early Egyptian are both around 3100 BCE; which is older is uncertain. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l80zyabM1gE?t=6468" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:47:48</a> | Old earth or young earth, and why? | He does not pick a side; the biblical text can be read more than one way, though common young-earth hermeneutics are problematic. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l80zyabM1gE?t=6669" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:51:09</a> | Is flat earth defensible? | No. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l80zyabM1gE?t=6699" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:51:39</a> | How could Manetho connect the Exodus to Thutmose? | He distrusts Manetho for Exodus reconstruction because the preserved versions vary and likely contain later insertions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l80zyabM1gE?t=7018" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:56:58</a> | How did ancient nations understand each other's languages? | Through multilingual scribes and diplomatic lingua francas such as Akkadian, later Aramaic and Greek. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l80zyabM1gE?t=7410" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:03:30</a> | Do you watch anime? | Yes; he is a longtime anime fan and lists older classics. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l80zyabM1gE?t=7551" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:05:51</a> | If Hazor was abandoned after its 13th-century destruction, where did Deborah's opponent rule from? | Hazor's monarchy may have survived elsewhere; Judges defeats the army, not necessarily the destroyed city. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l80zyabM1gE?t=7739" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:08:59</a> | Are there good Christian Mesopotamian scholars like Kitchen and Hoffmeier are for Egyptology? | He recommends Alan Millard and Paul Lawrence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l80zyabM1gE?t=7929" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:12:09</a> | Did you watch *Moon Knight* yet? | No, but he expects to watch it for a possible review. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l80zyabM1gE?t=7957" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:12:37</a> | What study Bible would you recommend? | Study Bibles vary; CSB and ESV have value, but he recommends comparing copies for fit. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l80zyabM1gE?t=8266" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:17:46</a> | What is going on with the altar laws in Exodus 20:22-26? | He reads them as separating Yahweh worship from surrounding pagan altar practices, especially cut stones and elevated approaches. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l80zyabM1gE?t=8679" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:24:39</a> | Should I worry that the ESV has a complementarian bias? | He is not worried about the translation itself, though he is more cautious about commentary notes. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l80zyabM1gE?t=8802" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:26:42</a> | Have you ever been to the Valley of Whales in Egypt? | No. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l80zyabM1gE?t=8850" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:27:30</a> | How many languages do you know? | He says he has reading competency in 13 and is working on a 14th. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l80zyabM1gE?t=9048" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:30:48</a> | What convinced you out of young-earth creationism? | Modern hermeneutics and reading the Bible in its ancient context. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l80zyabM1gE?t=9253" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:34:13</a> | Can you talk more about the "four loves in Greek" idea? | He argues the common sermon version is badly overstated; the Greek love words overlap far more than that popular teaching admits. |
